nodes.label: M1 design Codex adversarial review (20 ranked findings)
nodes.author: Codex (GPT-5.4)
nodes.rationale: Verdict: sound to implement with amendments, no structural rework — but the written design was underspecified enough to pass acceptance criteria while leaving disk exhaustion, orphaned containers, symlink jail escapes, Docker Desktop permission failures, CLI stdio corruption, and unenforced-but-claimed limits unresolved. Demanded a sharper contract.

nodes.label: Finding 4: killing the CLI is not killing the container
nodes.author: Codex (GPT-5.4)
nodes.rationale: On inner timeout, killing only the local docker CLI leaves the container running detached in the daemon while the handler reports a timeout. Use named containers; docker kill then rm -f under a separate cleanup deadline; --rm is a convenience, not the cleanup mechanism.

nodes.label: Finding 8: symlink jail escape via guest-planted links
nodes.author: Codex (GPT-5.4)
nodes.rationale: Guest code can plant a symlink/reparse point in the workspace pointing at host secrets; a naive resolve+prefix check on a later read_workspace call can be bypassed or race. Accept only relative paths, refuse symlinks anywhere in the chain, verify containment with path APIs not string prefix, document residual TOCTOU.

nodes.label: Finding 11: unspecified image strategy
nodes.author: Codex (GPT-5.4)
nodes.rationale: Without a pinned image strategy, :latest images behave differently next month or fail offline. Pin by digest for repeatability; preflight availability; never auto-pull during run_code.

nodes.label: Finding 13: isolation field too coarse for degraded container behavior
nodes.author: Codex (GPT-5.4)
nodes.rationale: Returning isolation: container while memory limits were silently ignored overclaims protection. Keep plain 'container' only for the full approved profile; add container-degraded and per-control detail.

nodes.label: Finding 19: output volume must be capped during collection, not at formatting
nodes.author: Codex (GPT-5.4)
nodes.rationale: A guest writing 10 GB to stdout harms the host before post-hoc truncation runs. Stream-read with byte caps and kill the container once caps are exceeded.
